# scripts\triage_bootstrap.py
@source-hash: 584f5c75957dfc87
@impl-hash: 56478213dcceeeb9
@generated: 2026-07-01T20:52:04Z

## Purpose

CLI bootstrap harness for the V1-4 Claim Builder (osojicode/work#27). Runs `osoji.triage.Triage` over a curated fixture set (`tests/fixtures/bootstrap/manifest.json`) in two modes — `explore` (evidence stripped, LLM uses tools to gather context) and `claim` (full evidence included) — measuring verdict accuracy vs. adjudicated labels and optionally computing baseline disagreement rate between runs.

## Path Constants (L54–63)

```
REPO_ROOT       = <repo root>          # scripts/../
BOOTSTRAP_DIR   = REPO_ROOT/tests/fixtures/bootstrap
DEFAULT_MANIFEST = BOOTSTRAP_DIR/manifest.json
DEFAULT_TRACE_DIR = BOOTSTRAP_DIR/traces
```

`REPO_ROOT` is inserted into `sys.path` at L55 so `osoji.*` imports work without installation.

## Key Functions

### `load_manifest(path)` (L66–72)
Reads and validates `manifest.json`. Raises `ValueError` if any entry lacks `slug`, `category`, `adjudicated_verdict`, or `finding`. Returns raw `dict` with top-level `commit` and `entries` list.

### `entries_to_claims(entries, *, strip_evidence)` (L75–84)
Converts manifest entries to `Claim` objects via `Finding.from_dict(entry["finding"])`. When `strip_evidence=True` (explore mode), uses `dataclasses.replace` to zero out `finding.evidence` before wrapping in `Claim`. This ensures explore traces reflect LLM-driven retrieval rather than pre-assembled evidence.

### `summarize(entries, findings)` (L87–116)
Zips manifest entries with triage-output `Finding` objects (must be same order). Builds per-category `{n, agree, uncertain}` stats and per-row rows. Returns summary dict with `n`, `accuracy`, `per_category`, `rows`. Uses `finding.verdict == entry["adjudicated_verdict"]` for agreement; `finding.verdict == "uncertain"` counts uncertain verdicts separately.

### `run(args)` (L119–180) — async
Main orchestration:
1. Loads manifest, optionally filters by `args.only` sl

# tests\conftest.py
@source-hash: 8a12e677de9afca2
@impl-hash: 56478213dcceeeb9
@generated: 2026-07-01T20:29:41Z

## `tests/conftest.py` — Shared Pytest Fixtures

Provides project-wide pytest infrastructure: a CLI flag for baseline establishment, an autouse cache-clearing fixture, and a temp directory factory.

### Fixtures

**`_clear_repo_files_cache`** (L26–31) — `autouse=True`, scope=function. Calls `clear_repo_files_cache()` from `osoji.walker` both before the test starts and after it completes (teardown via `yield`). Ensures the git `ls-files` cache never leaks between tests regardless of test isolation failures.

**`temp_dir`** (L34–39) — Yields a `pathlib.Path` pointing to a fresh directory created via `tempfile.mkdtemp()`. Deletes the directory tree on teardown (`shutil.rmtree(..., ignore_errors=True)`). Tests requesting this fixture get a unique directory per invocation. Per project convention this fixture is preferred over pytest's built-in `tmp_path` in this codebase (both exist; project uses `temp_dir`).

**`establish_baseline`** (L22–23) — Reads the `--establish-baseline` CLI flag from the pytest config and returns its boolean value. Consumed by prompt regression tests to switch between assertion mode and baseline-writing mode.

### CLI Hook

**`pytest_addoption`** (L12–18) — Registers `--establish-baseline` (store_true, default False) with the pytest argument parser. This is a pytest hook, not a fixture, so it runs at collection time.

### Key Dependency

- `osoji.walker.clear_repo_files_cache` — imported and called in the autouse fixture. Any test that indirectly populates the walker cache will have it cleared automatically.

### Design Notes

- `_clear_repo_files_cache` is underscore-prefixed but still a pytest fixture (visible to pytest via `autouse=True`); the underscore signals it is not intended for direct injection in test signatures.
- `temp_dir` uses manual `mkdtemp` + `rmtree` rather than pytest's `tmp_path` — this is an intentional project c
